title: GNSS resilience is an economic and security priority
summary:
Modern society has become profoundly reliant on Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS). These systems support aviation safety, emergency services, finance, communications, energy networks and an expanding array of autonomous and industrial systems. Yet despite this reliance, GNSS remains inherently fragile: low‑power signals transmitted from medium Earth orbit are surprisingly easy to degrade, and the consequences […]

title: Voyager Technologies invests in Max Space
summary:
Voyager Technologies is investing in Max Space to help accelerate a partnership between the two companies on developing lunar habitats.

title: First Starship V3 launch slips
summary:
SpaceX is pushing back the first launch of the latest version of Starship even as NASA is asking it to accelerate work on a lunar lander version of the vehicle.

title: From Cabbages to Countdowns: NASA Marks 100 Years of Modern Rocketry
summary: Snow covered the ground that Tuesday morning 100 years ago, when a college professor and his wife took a morning drive to the family farm a few miles south in Auburn, Massachusetts. title: Chinese official calls for prioritizing Neptune orbiter mission
summary:
A senior Chinese space scientist and delegate to the country’s national congress is proposing the prioritization of an unprecedented orbiter mission to ice giant Neptune.

title: Smile arrives at Europe’s Spaceport
summary: The Smile spacecraft has arrived at Europe’s Spaceport in French Guiana. During the coming weeks, the spacecraft will go through final preparations for its launch on a Vega-C rocket between 8 April and 7 May.

title: The radiation paradox: why solar maximum is the safest time to travel to Mars
summary: When packing your spacecraft for a return trip to Mars, it would be better to launch during a period of maximum solar activity.

title: ESA analysing fireball over Europe on 8 March 2026
summary: At approximately 18:55 CET (17:55 UTC) on Sunday 8 March 2026, a very bright fireball moving from the southwest to the northeast was observed by many people in Belgium, France, Germany, Luxembourg, and the Netherlands.

title: "She flies satellites. One day, I can too."
summary: At ESA’s European Space Operations Centre (ESOC), teams work around the clock to fly spacecraft across the Solar System and monitor Earth from orbit. Among them are women leading spacecraft operations, managing teams and helping shape the culture of ESA’s mission control.

title: Hostage to the Moon - How Artemis Became Industrial Welfare in a Space Suit - Part 2
summary:
Los Angeles CA (SPX) Mar 09, 2026

NASA says it has finally found the "back to basics" recipe to get Americans back on the Moon by 2028. A new intermediate mission, standardized hardware, a faster launch cadence: on paper, the Artemis overhaul looks like a sober course correction after years of drift.
